#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Outbid Dirigent – Analyzer
Analysiert Repo-Struktur und Spec-Inhalt um den optimalen Ausführungspfad zu bestimmen.
"""

import os
import re
import json
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict
from collections import Counter

from outbid_dirigent.logger import get_logger


@dataclass
class RepoAnalysis:
    """Ergebnis der Repository-Analyse."""
    repo_path: str
    repo_name: str
    primary_language: str
    secondary_languages: List[str]
    framework_detected: Optional[str]
    build_tool: Optional[str]
    commit_count: int
    last_commit_days_ago: int
    last_commit_date: Optional[str]
    file_count: int
    total_lines: int
    has_tests: bool
    has_ci: bool
    directories: List[str]
    config_files: List[str]


@dataclass
class SpecAnalysis:
    """Ergebnis der Spec-Analyse."""
    spec_path: str
    title: str
    has_legacy_keywords: bool
    has_greenfield_keywords: bool
    has_testability_keywords: bool
    has_tracking_keywords: bool
    legacy_keywords_found: List[str]
    greenfield_keywords_found: List[str]
    testability_keywords_found: List[str]
    tracking_keywords_found: List[str]
    target_language: Optional[str]
    complexity: Optional[str]
    estimated_scope: str  # small, medium, large


@dataclass
class RuntimeRequirement:
    """Eine benötigte Runtime-Abhängigkeit (Service, Tool, etc.)."""
    name: str
    type: str  # database, cache, queue, storage, api
    docker_image: Optional[str] = None
    port: Optional[int] = None
    env_vars: Optional[List[str]] = None


@dataclass
class RuntimeAnalysis:
    """Analyse wie das Projekt lokal gestartet wird."""
    start_command: str  # z.B. "npm run dev"
    port: int  # z.B. 3000
    package_manager: str  # npm, yarn, pnpm, pip, go, etc.
    framework: Optional[str]  # Next.js, FastAPI, etc.
    services: List[RuntimeRequirement]  # Benötigte Services (DB, Redis, etc.)
    setup_steps: List[str]  # z.B. ["npm install", "npm run db:migrate"]
    uses_doppler: bool  # Ob Doppler für Secrets verwendet wird
    uses_docker_compose: bool  # Ob docker-compose.yml vorhanden ist
    env_file: Optional[str]  # .env.example oder .env.local
    health_check_path: Optional[str]  # z.B. "/api/health"


@dataclass
class AnalysisResult:
    """Kombiniertes Analyse-Ergebnis."""
    repo: RepoAnalysis
    spec: SpecAnalysis
    runtime: Optional[RuntimeAnalysis]  # NEU: Runtime-Analyse
    route: str  # greenfield, legacy, hybrid
    route_reason: str
    confidence: str  # low, medium, high
    legacy_signals: int
    greenfield_signals: int


# Sprach-Erkennung basierend auf Dateiendungen
LANGUAGE_EXTENSIONS = {
    ".py": "Python",
    ".js": "JavaScript",
    ".ts": "TypeScript",
    ".tsx": "TypeScript",
    ".jsx": "JavaScript",
    ".java": "Java",
    ".kt": "Kotlin",
    ".rb": "Ruby",
    ".php": "PHP",
    ".go": "Go",
    ".rs": "Rust",
    ".swift": "Swift",
    ".m": "Objective-C",
    ".cs": "C#",
    ".cpp": "C++",
    ".c": "C",
    ".scala": "Scala",
    ".clj": "Clojure",
    ".ex": "Elixir",
    ".exs": "Elixir",
    ".hs": "Haskell",
    ".lua": "Lua",
    ".r": "R",
    ".jl": "Julia",
    ".dart": "Dart",
    ".vue": "Vue",
    ".svelte": "Svelte",
}

# Framework-Erkennung basierend auf Dateien
FRAMEWORK_INDICATORS = {
    "package.json": {
        "react": "React",
        "vue": "Vue.js",
        "angular": "Angular",
        "next": "Next.js",
        "nuxt": "Nuxt.js",
        "express": "Express.js",
        "fastify": "Fastify",
        "nest": "NestJS",
        "svelte": "Svelte",
    },
    "requirements.txt": {
        "django": "Django",
        "flask": "Flask",
        "fastapi": "FastAPI",
        "tornado": "Tornado",
    },
    "Gemfile": {
        "rails": "Ruby on Rails",
        "sinatra": "Sinatra",
    },
    "composer.json": {
        "laravel": "Laravel",
        "symfony": "Symfony",
    },
    "pom.xml": {
        "spring": "Spring Boot",
    },
    "build.gradle": {
        "spring": "Spring Boot",
    },
    "go.mod": {},
    "Cargo.toml": {},
    "pubspec.yaml": {
        "flutter": "Flutter",
    },
}

# Build-Tool-Erkennung
BUILD_TOOLS = {
    "package.json": "npm/yarn",
    "pom.xml": "Maven",
    "build.gradle": "Gradle",
    "Makefile": "Make",
    "CMakeLists.txt": "CMake",
    "Cargo.toml": "Cargo",
    "go.mod": "Go Modules",
    "requirements.txt": "pip",
    "Pipfile": "Pipenv",
    "pyproject.toml": "Poetry/pip",
    "Gemfile": "Bundler",
    "composer.json": "Composer",
    "pubspec.yaml": "pub",
}

# Keywords für Routing-Entscheidung
LEGACY_KEYWORDS = [
    "refactor", "migrate", "migration", "rewrite", "port", "legacy",
    "convert", "modernize", "upgrade", "replace", "deprecated",
    "technical debt", "tech debt", "overhaul", "replatform",
    "umschreiben", "migrieren", "portieren", "ersetzen", "modernisieren",
]

GREENFIELD_KEYWORDS = [
    "add", "build", "create", "implement", "new feature", "neue",
    "develop", "design", "introduce", "setup", "initialize",
    "hinzufügen", "erstellen", "bauen", "implementieren", "entwickeln",
    "feature", "neu", "anlegen",
]

TESTABILITY_KEYWORDS = [
    "testability", "testbarkeit", "test coverage", "testabdeckung",

1	"""
2	Plan schema — typed representation of PLAN.json.
3	
4	Uses pydantic for validation, serialization, and deserialization.
5	"""
6	
7	import json
8	from pathlib import Path
9	from typing import Optional
10	
11	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
12	
13	
14	class Task(BaseModel):
15	    """A single executable task within a phase."""
16	    id: str
17	    name: str
18	    description: str = ""
19	    files_to_create: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
20	    files_to_modify: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
21	    depends_on: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
22	    model: str = ""
23	    effort: str = ""
24	    test_level: str = ""  # "", "L1", "L2"
25	    convention_skills: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)  # e.g. ["ruby-code-writing", "form-builder"]
26	
27	
28	class Phase(BaseModel):
29	    """A group of related tasks executed sequentially."""
30	    id: str
31	    name: str
32	    description: str = ""
33	    tasks: list[Task] = Field(default_factory=list)
34	
35	    def model_post_init(self, __context):
36	        # Normalize: accept both "id" and "phase" field names
37	        if not self.id and hasattr(self, "phase"):
38	            self.id = str(getattr(self, "phase"))
39	
40	
41	class Plan(BaseModel):
42	    """The full execution plan for a dirigent run."""
43	    title: str = "Untitled"
44	    summary: str = ""
45	    phases: list[Phase] = Field(default_factory=list)
46	    estimated_complexity: str = "medium"
47	    risks: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
48	    assumptions: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
49	    out_of_scope: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
50	
51	    @property
52	    def total_tasks(self) -> int:
53	        return sum(len(p.tasks) for p in self.phases)
54	
55	    @property
56	    def all_tasks(self) -> list[tuple[Task, Phase]]:
57	        """Flat list of (task, phase) in execution order."""
58	        return [(t, p) for p in self.phases for t in p.tasks]
59	
60	    def task_position(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
61	        """Position info for a task: index, total, phase, prev/next."""
62	        tasks = self.all_tasks
63	        idx = next((i for i, (t, _) in enumerate(tasks) if t.id == task_id), None)
64	        if idx is None:
65	            return None
66	
67	        t, p = tasks[idx]
68	        info = {
69	            "index": idx + 1,
70	            "total": len(tasks),
71	            "phase_id": p.id,
72	            "phase_name": p.name,
73	            "total_phases": len(self.phases),
74	        }
75	        if idx > 0:
76	            prev_t, _ = tasks[idx - 1]
77	            info["prev_id"] = prev_t.id
78	            info["prev_name"] = prev_t.name
79	        if idx < len(tasks) - 1:
80	            next_t, _ = tasks[idx + 1]
81	            info["next_id"] = next_t.id
82	            info["next_name"] = next_t.name
83	        return info
84	
85	    def save(self, path: Path):
86	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
87	        path.write_text(
88	            self.model_dump_json(indent=2, exclude_none=True),
89	            encoding="utf-8",
90	        )
91	
92	    @classmethod
93	    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Optional["Plan"]:
94	        """Load from PLAN.json. Returns None if missing or invalid."""
95	        if not path.exists():
96	            return None
97	        try:
98	            raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
99	            # Normalize phase id field
100	            for p in raw.get("phases", []):
101	                if "phase" in p and "id" not in p:
102	                    p["id"] = str(p.pop("phase"))
103	                elif "id" in p:
104	                    p["id"] = str(p["id"])
105	            return cls.model_validate(raw)
106	        except Exception:
107	            return None
108	
109	    @staticmethod
110	    def json_template() -> str:
111	        """JSON template string for the plan creation prompt."""
112	        return """{
113	  "title": "Feature-Titel",
114	  "summary": "Kurze Beschreibung was implementiert wird",
115	  "assumptions": [
116	    "Annahmen die du über die Codebase/das Feature machst",
117	    "z.B. 'Tests laufen mit pytest', 'API ist REST-basiert'"
118	  ],
119	  "out_of_scope": [
120	    "Was NICHT Teil dieses Plans ist",
121	    "z.B. 'Deployment/CI-Pipeline', 'Performance-Optimierung'"
122	  ],
123	  "phases": [
124	    {
125	      "id": "01",
126	      "name": "Phase-Name",
127	      "description": "Was in dieser Phase passiert",
128	      "tasks": [
129	        {
130	          "id": "01-01",
131	          "name": "Task-Name",
132	          "description": "Detaillierte Beschreibung was zu tun ist",
133	          "files_to_create": ["liste/von/neuen/dateien.ext"],
134	          "files_to_modify": ["liste/von/existierenden/dateien.ext"],
135	          "depends_on": [],
136	          "model": "sonnet|haiku|opus (welches Modell für diesen Task am besten ist)",
137	          "effort": "low|medium|high (wie viel Denkaufwand nötig ist)",
138	          "test_level": "L1|L2| (welches Test-Level nach diesem Task laufen soll, leer wenn kein Test nötig)",
139	          "convention_skills": ["skill-name-1", "skill-name-2 (aus .opencode/skills/, leer wenn keine)"]
140	        }
141	      ]
142	    }
143	  ],
144	  "estimated_complexity": "low|medium|high",
145	  "risks": ["Liste von potentiellen Risiken"]
146	}"""

1	"""
2	Contract & Review schemas — typed representations for phase contracts and reviews.
3	
4	Contracts define acceptance criteria before a phase starts.
5	Reviews evaluate changes against those criteria after execution.
6	Both are JSON files validated by Pydantic so Python never string-matches markdown.
7	"""
8	
9	import json
10	from enum import Enum
11	from pathlib import Path
12	from typing import Optional
13	
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
15	
16	
17	# ══════════════════════════════════════════
18	# ENUMS
19	# ══════════════════════════════════════════
20	
21	class CriterionLayer(str, Enum):
22	    STRUCTURAL = "structural"   # File exists, compiles, router registered
23	    BEHAVIORAL = "behavioral"   # Feature works: HTTP calls, auth, data persistence
24	    BOUNDARY = "boundary"       # Edge cases: error paths, malformed input, duplicates
25	
26	
27	class CriterionVerdict(str, Enum):
28	    PASS = "pass"
29	    FAIL = "fail"
30	    WARN = "warn"
31	
32	
33	class Verdict(str, Enum):
34	    PASS = "pass"
35	    FAIL = "fail"
36	
37	
38	class FindingSeverity(str, Enum):
39	    CRITICAL = "critical"
40	    WARN = "warn"
41	    INFO = "info"
42	
43	
44	# ══════════════════════════════════════════
45	# CONTRACT SCHEMA
46	# ══════════════════════════════════════════
47	
48	class AcceptanceCriterion(BaseModel):
49	    """A single measurable acceptance criterion."""
50	    id: str = Field(..., description="Unique ID like AC-01-01")
51	    description: str = Field(..., description="What must be true")
52	    verification: str = Field(..., description="Executable command to verify (Run: ...)")
53	    layer: CriterionLayer = CriterionLayer.BEHAVIORAL
54	
55	
56	class ExpectedFileChange(BaseModel):
57	    """A file expected to change during the phase."""
58	    path: str
59	    change: str = Field(..., description="What changes in this file")
60	
61	
62	class Contract(BaseModel):
63	    """Phase contract — acceptance criteria agreed before execution."""
64	    phase_id: str
65	    phase_name: str
66	    objective: str = Field(..., description="One-sentence phase objective")
67	    acceptance_criteria: list[AcceptanceCriterion] = Field(
68	        ..., min_length=1, max_length=8,
69	        description="Measurable criteria (1-8)"
70	    )
71	    quality_gates: list[str] = Field(
72	        default_factory=lambda: [
73	            "All new/modified files compile without errors",
74	            "No regressions in existing functionality",
75	            "Code follows project conventions",
76	        ]
77	    )
78	    out_of_scope: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
79	    expected_files: list[ExpectedFileChange] = Field(default_factory=list)
80	
81	    def save(self, path: Path):
82	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
83	        path.write_text(
84	            self.model_dump_json(indent=2),
85	            encoding="utf-8",
86	        )
87	
88	    @classmethod
89	    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Optional["Contract"]:
90	        if not path.exists():
91	            return None
92	        try:
93	            raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
94	            # Backward compat: map old category field to new layer field
95	            for criterion in raw.get("acceptance_criteria", []):
96	                if "category" in criterion and "layer" not in criterion:
97	                    category = criterion.pop("category")
98	                    criterion["layer"] = {
99	                        "functional": "behavioral",
100	                        "quality": "structural",
101	                    }.get(category, "behavioral")
102	            return cls.model_validate(raw)
103	        except Exception:
104	            return None
105	
106	    @staticmethod
107	    def json_template() -> str:
108	        return """{
109	  "phase_id": "01",
110	  "phase_name": "Phase Name",
111	  "objective": "One sentence: what this phase achieves",
112	  "acceptance_criteria": [
113	    {
114	      "id": "AC-01-01",
115	      "description": "Project compiles without errors",
116	      "verification": "Run: npm run build",
117	      "layer": "structural"
118	    },
119	    {
120	      "id": "AC-01-02",
121	      "description": "GET /api/users returns list of users with correct schema",
122	      "verification": "Run: curl -sf http://localhost:3000/api/users -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' | jq '.[] | keys'",
123	      "layer": "behavioral"
124	    },
125	    {
126	      "id": "AC-01-03",
127	      "description": "Unauthorized request returns 403",
128	      "verification": "Run: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3000/api/admin/users",
129	      "layer": "boundary"
130	    }
131	  ],
132	  "quality_gates": [
133	    "All new/modified files compile without errors",
134	    "No regressions in existing functionality",
135	    "Code follows project conventions"
136	  ],
137	  "out_of_scope": ["What this phase does NOT cover"],
138	  "expected_files": [
139	    {"path": "src/foo.py", "change": "Add new class"}
140	  ]
141	}"""
142	
143	    def summary_for_prompt(self) -> str:
144	        """Compact summary for injection into task prompts — includes verification commands."""
145	        criteria = "\n".join(
146	            f"  - [{c.id}] ({c.layer.value}) {c.description}\n"
147	            f"    Verify: {c.verification}"
148	            for c in self.acceptance_criteria
149	        )
150	        return (
151	            f"Phase {self.phase_id} Contract: {self.objective}\n"
152	            f"Acceptance Criteria:\n{criteria}"
153	        )

157	# REVIEW SCHEMA
158	# ══════════════════════════════════════════
159	
160	class VerificationEvidence(BaseModel):
161	    """Proof that a verification command was actually executed."""
162	    command: str = Field(..., description="The command that was run")
163	    exit_code: int = Field(..., description="Exit code of the command")
164	    stdout_snippet: str = Field("", description="First 500 chars of stdout")
165	    stderr_snippet: str = Field("", description="First 500 chars of stderr")
166	
167	
168	class CriterionResult(BaseModel):
169	    """Evaluation of a single acceptance criterion."""
170	    ac_id: str = Field(..., description="References AcceptanceCriterion.id")
171	    verdict: CriterionVerdict
172	    notes: str = ""
173	    evidence: list[VerificationEvidence] = Field(
174	        default_factory=list,
175	        description="Commands run to verify this criterion. Required for functional criteria.",
176	    )
177	    verification_tier: str = ""  # InfraTier value used to verify this criterion
178	
179	
180	class Finding(BaseModel):
181	    """A code quality finding from the review."""
182	    severity: FindingSeverity
183	    file: str
184	    line: int = 0
185	    description: str
186	    suggestion: str = ""
187	
188	
189	class Review(BaseModel):
190	    """Phase review — evaluation of changes against the contract."""
191	    phase_id: str
192	    iteration: int = 1
193	    verdict: Verdict
194	    confidence: str = "static"     # e2e | integration | unit | mocked | static | none
195	    infra_tier: str = "7_none"      # InfraTier value active during execution
196	    tests_run: int = 0
197	    tests_skipped_infra: int = 0
198	    caveat: str = ""                # human-readable explanation of what wasn't verified
199	    criteria_results: list[CriterionResult] = Field(default_factory=list)
200	    findings: list[Finding] = Field(default_factory=list)
201	    summary: str = ""
202	
203	    @property
204	    def critical_count(self) -> int:
205	        return sum(1 for f in self.findings if f.severity == FindingSeverity.CRITICAL)
206	
207	    @property
208	    def warn_count(self) -> int:
209	        return sum(1 for f in self.findings if f.severity == FindingSeverity.WARN)
210	
211	    @property
212	    def failed_criteria(self) -> list[CriterionResult]:
213	        return [r for r in self.criteria_results if r.verdict == CriterionVerdict.FAIL]
214	
215	    @property
216	    def passed_criteria(self) -> list[CriterionResult]:
217	        return [r for r in self.criteria_results if r.verdict == CriterionVerdict.PASS]
218	
219	    @property
220	    def criteria_without_evidence(self) -> list[CriterionResult]:
221	        """Criteria marked as pass but with no verification evidence."""
222	        return [
223	            r for r in self.criteria_results
224	            if r.verdict == CriterionVerdict.PASS and not r.evidence
225	        ]
226	
227	    def save(self, path: Path):
228	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
229	        path.write_text(
230	            self.model_dump_json(indent=2),
231	            encoding="utf-8",
232	        )
233	
234	    @classmethod
235	    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Optional["Review"]:
236	        if not path.exists():
237	            return None
238	        try:
239	            raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
240	            # Backward compat: old schema used uppercase verdict, different field names
241	            if "verdict" in raw and isinstance(raw["verdict"], str):
242	                raw["verdict"] = raw["verdict"].lower()
243	            if "results" in raw and "criteria_results" not in raw:
244	                # Old field was "results" with id/status/layer/actual/notes
245	                old_results = raw.pop("results")
246	                raw["criteria_results"] = [
247	                    {
248	                        "ac_id": r.get("id", ""),
249	                        "verdict": r.get("status", "fail").lower(),
250	                        "notes": r.get("actual") or r.get("notes") or "",
251	                    }
252	                    for r in old_results
253	                ]
254	            if "issues" in raw and "findings" not in raw:
255	                _sev_map = {"low": "info", "medium": "warn", "high": "critical"}
256	                raw["findings"] = [
257	                    {
258	                        "severity": _sev_map.get(i.get("severity", "info"), i.get("severity", "info")),
259	                        "file": i.get("criterion", ""),
260	                        "line": 0,
261	                        "description": i.get("description", ""),
262	                        "suggestion": i.get("recommendation", ""),
263	                    }
264	                    for i in raw.pop("issues")
265	                ]
266	            return cls.model_validate(raw)
267	        except Exception:
268	            return None
269	
270	    @staticmethod
271	    def json_template() -> str:
272	        return """{
273	  "phase_id": "01",
274	  "iteration": 1,
275	  "verdict": "pass or fail",
276	  "criteria_results": [
277	    {
278	      "ac_id": "AC-01-01",
279	      "verdict": "pass",
280	      "notes": "Criterion met because..."
281	    },
282	    {
283	      "ac_id": "AC-01-02",
284	      "verdict": "fail",
285	      "notes": "Not met because..."
286	    }
287	  ],
288	  "findings": [
289	    {
290	      "severity": "critical",
291	      "file": "src/foo.py",
292	      "line": 42,
293	      "description": "Null check missing",
294	      "suggestion": "Add if x is None guard"
295	    }
296	  ],
297	  "summary": "Overall assessment"
298	}"""

247	class Analyzer:
248	    """Analysiert Repository und Spec für die Routing-Entscheidung."""
249	
250	    def __init__(self, repo_path: str, spec_path: str):
251	        self.repo_path = Path(repo_path).resolve()
252	        self.spec_path = Path(spec_path).resolve()
253	        self.logger = get_logger()
254	
255	    def analyze(self) -> AnalysisResult:
256	        """Führt die komplette Analyse durch."""
257	        self.logger.analyze(self.repo_path.name)
258	
259	        repo_analysis = self._analyze_repo()
260	        spec_analysis = self._analyze_spec()
261	        runtime_analysis = self._analyze_runtime(repo_analysis)
262	
263	        # Log Statistiken
264	        age_info = f"{repo_analysis.last_commit_days_ago} Tage seit letztem Commit"
265	        if repo_analysis.last_commit_days_ago > 365:
266	            years = repo_analysis.last_commit_days_ago // 365
267	            age_info = f"{years} Jahr{'e' if years > 1 else ''} alt"
268	
269	        self.logger.stats(
270	            f"{repo_analysis.primary_language}/{repo_analysis.framework_detected or 'kein Framework'}",
271	            repo_analysis.commit_count,
272	            age_info
273	        )
274	
275	        # Log Runtime-Info
276	        if runtime_analysis:
277	            self.logger.debug(
278	                f"Runtime: {runtime_analysis.start_command} (Port {runtime_analysis.port}), "
279	                f"{len(runtime_analysis.services)} Services, Doppler: {runtime_analysis.uses_doppler}"
280	            )
281	
282	        # Route bestimmen
283	        route, reason, confidence, legacy_signals, greenfield_signals = self._determine_route(
284	            repo_analysis, spec_analysis
285	        )
286	
287	        self.logger.route(route, confidence)
288	        self.logger.reason(reason)
289	
290	        result = AnalysisResult(
291	            repo=repo_analysis,
292	            spec=spec_analysis,
293	            runtime=runtime_analysis,
294	            route=route,
295	            route_reason=reason,
296	            confidence=confidence,
297	            legacy_signals=legacy_signals,
298	            greenfield_signals=greenfield_signals,
299	        )
300	
301	        # Speichere Analyse
302	        self._save_analysis(result)
303	
304	        return result
305	
306	    def _analyze_repo(self) -> RepoAnalysis:
307	        """Analysiert die Repository-Struktur."""
308	        # Dateien sammeln
309	        all_files = self._get_all_files()
310	
311	        # Sprachen erkennen
312	        primary_lang, secondary_langs = self._detect_languages(all_files)
313	
314	        # Framework erkennen
315	        framework = self._detect_framework()
316	
317	        # Build-Tool erkennen
318	        build_tool = self._detect_build_tool()
319	
320	        # Git-Statistiken
321	        commit_count, last_commit_days, last_commit_date = self._get_git_stats()
322	
323	        # Codezeilen zählen (approximativ)
324	        total_lines = self._count_lines(all_files)
325	
326	        # Verzeichnisse
327	        directories = self._get_top_directories()
328	
329	        # Config-Dateien
330	        config_files = self._get_config_files()
331	
332	        # Tests vorhanden?
333	        has_tests = self._has_tests()
334	
335	        # CI vorhanden?
336	        has_ci = self._has_ci()
337	
338	        return RepoAnalysis(
339	            repo_path=str(self.repo_path),
340	            repo_name=self.repo_path.name,
341	            primary_language=primary_lang,
342	            secondary_languages=secondary_langs,
343	            framework_detected=framework,
344	            build_tool=build_tool,
345	            commit_count=commit_count,
346	            last_commit_days_ago=last_commit_days,
347	            last_commit_date=last_commit_date,
348	            file_count=len(all_files),
349	            total_lines=total_lines,
350	            has_tests=has_tests,
351	            has_ci=has_ci,
352	            directories=directories,
353	            config_files=config_files,
354	        )
355	
356	    def _get_all_files(self) -> List[Path]:
357	        """Sammelt alle Dateien im Repo (ohne .git, node_modules, etc.)."""
358	        exclude_dirs = {
359	            ".git", "node_modules", "vendor", "venv", ".venv",
360	            "__pycache__", ".idea", ".vscode", "dist", "build",
361	            "target", ".gradle", ".dirigent",
362	        }
363	
364	        files = []
365	        for root, dirs, filenames in os.walk(self.repo_path):
366	            # Exclude-Verzeichnisse entfernen
367	            dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if d not in exclude_dirs]
368	
369	            for filename in filenames:
370	                if not filename.startswith("."):
371	                    files.append(Path(root) / filename)
372	
373	        return files
374	
375	    def _detect_languages(self, files: List[Path]) -> Tuple[str, List[str]]:
376	        """Erkennt primäre und sekundäre Sprachen."""
377	        lang_counts: Counter = Counter()
378	
379	        for file in files:
380	            ext = file.suffix.lower()
381	            if ext in LANGUAGE_EXTENSIONS:
382	                lang_counts[LANGUAGE_EXTENSIONS[ext]] += 1
383	
384	        if not lang_counts:
385	            return "Unknown", []
386	
387	        sorted_langs = lang_counts.most_common()
388	        primary = sorted_langs[0][0]
389	        secondary = [lang for lang, _ in sorted_langs[1:4]]
390	
391	        return primary, secondary
392	
393	    def _detect_framework(self) -> Optional[str]:
394	        """Erkennt das verwendete Framework."""
395	        for config_file, frameworks in FRAMEWORK_INDICATORS.items():
396	            config_path = self.repo_path / config_file
397	            if config_path.exists():
398	                try:
399	                    content = config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()
400	                    for keyword, framework in frameworks.items():
401	                        if keyword in content:
402	                            return framework
403	                except Exception:
404	                    pass
405	
406	        # Spezielle Checks
407	        if (self.repo_path / "pom.xml").exists():
408	            try:
409	                content = (self.repo_path / "pom.xml").read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()
410	                if "spring-boot" in content or "springframework" in content:
411	                    return "Spring Boot"
412	            except Exception:
413	                pass
414	
415	        return None
416	
417	    def _detect_build_tool(self) -> Optional[str]:
418	        """Erkennt das Build-Tool."""
419	        for filename, tool in BUILD_TOOLS.items():
420	            if (self.repo_path / filename).exists():
421	                return tool
422	        return None
423	
424	    def _get_git_stats(self) -> Tuple[int, int, Optional[str]]:
425	        """Holt Git-Statistiken."""
426	        try:
427	            # Commit-Anzahl
428	            result = subprocess.run(
429	                ["git", "rev-list", "--count", "HEAD"],
430	                cwd=self.repo_path,
431	                capture_output=True,
432	                text=True,
433	            )
434	            commit_count = int(result.stdout.strip()) if result.returncode == 0 else 0
435	
436	            # Letzter Commit
437	            result = subprocess.run(
438	                ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%ci"],
439	                cwd=self.repo_path,
440	                capture_output=True,
441	                text=True,
442	            )
443	            if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
444	                last_commit_str = result.stdout.strip()
445	                # Parse: "2024-01-15 10:30:00 +0100"
446	                last_commit_date = last_commit_str.split()[0]
447	                last_commit = datetime.fromisoformat(last_commit_date)
448	                days_ago = (datetime.now() - last_commit).days
449	                return commit_count, days_ago, last_commit_date
450	
451	            return commit_count, 0, None
452	
453	        except Exception:
454	            return 0, 0, None
455	
456	    def _count_lines(self, files: List[Path]) -> int:
457	        """Zählt Codezeilen (approximativ)."""
458	        total = 0
459	        code_extensions = set(LANGUAGE_EXTENSIONS.keys())
460	
461	        for file in files[:500]:  # Limit für Performance
462	            if file.suffix.lower() in code_extensions:
463	                try:
464	                    total += len(file.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines())
465	                except Exception:
466	                    pass
467	
468	        return total
469	
470	    def _get_top_directories(self) -> List[str]:
471	        """Gibt die wichtigsten Verzeichnisse zurück."""
472	        important_dirs = ["src", "app", "lib", "api", "components", "pages",
473	                         "ios", "android", "server", "client", "core", "modules"]
474	
475	        found = []
476	        for d in self.repo_path.iterdir():
477	            if d.is_dir() and not d.name.startswith("."):
478	                if d.name in important_dirs:
479	                    found.append(d.name)
480	
481	        return found
482	
483	    def _get_config_files(self) -> List[str]:
484	        """Gibt gefundene Konfigurationsdateien zurück."""
485	        config_patterns = [
486	            "package.json", "pom.xml", "build.gradle", "Cargo.toml",
487	            "go.mod", "requirements.txt", "Gemfile", "composer.json",
488	            "tsconfig.json", "webpack.config.js", "vite.config.js",
489	            ".env", ".env.example", "docker-compose.yml", "Dockerfile",
490	        ]
491	
492	        found = []
493	        for pattern in config_patterns:
494	            if (self.repo_path / pattern).exists():
495	                found.append(pattern)
496	
497	        return found
498	
499	    def _has_tests(self) -> bool:
500	        """Prüft ob Tests vorhanden sind."""
501	        test_indicators = [
502	            "test", "tests", "spec", "specs", "__tests__",
503	            "pytest.ini", "jest.config.js", "vitest.config.js",
504	        ]
505	
506	        for d in self.repo_path.iterdir():
507	            if d.name.lower() in test_indicators:
508	                return True
509	
510	        return False
511	
512	    def _has_ci(self) -> bool:
513	        """Prüft ob CI-Konfiguration vorhanden ist."""
514	        ci_paths = [
515	            ".github/workflows",
516	            ".gitlab-ci.yml",
517	            ".circleci",

775	    def _analyze_spec(self) -> SpecAnalysis:
776	        """Analysiert die Spec-Datei."""
777	        content = self.spec_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
778	        content_lower = content.lower()
779	
780	        # Titel extrahieren (erste H1 Zeile)
781	        title = "Unbekannt"
782	        for line in content.splitlines():
783	            if line.startswith("# "):
784	                title = line[2:].strip()
785	                break
786	
787	        # Legacy-Keywords suchen
788	        legacy_found = []
789	        for keyword in LEGACY_KEYWORDS:
790	            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
791	                legacy_found.append(keyword)
792	
793	        # Greenfield-Keywords suchen
794	        greenfield_found = []
795	        for keyword in GREENFIELD_KEYWORDS:
796	            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
797	                greenfield_found.append(keyword)
798	
799	        # Testability-Keywords suchen
800	        testability_found = []
801	        for keyword in TESTABILITY_KEYWORDS:
802	            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
803	                testability_found.append(keyword)
804	
805	        # Tracking-Keywords suchen
806	        tracking_found = []
807	        for keyword in TRACKING_KEYWORDS:
808	            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
809	                tracking_found.append(keyword)
810	
811	        # Target-Language erkennen
812	        target_language = None
813	        for keyword, lang in LANGUAGE_KEYWORDS.items():
814	            if keyword in content_lower:
815	                target_language = lang
816	                break
817	
818	        # Komplexität aus Spec lesen
819	        complexity = None
820	        complexity_match = re.search(r"complexity:\s*(\w+)", content_lower)
821	        if complexity_match:
822	            complexity = complexity_match.group(1)
823	
824	        # Scope schätzen
825	        word_count = len(content.split())
826	        if word_count < 200:
827	            scope = "small"
828	        elif word_count < 500:
829	            scope = "medium"
830	        else:
831	            scope = "large"
832	
833	        return SpecAnalysis(
834	            spec_path=str(self.spec_path),
835	            title=title,
836	            has_legacy_keywords=len(legacy_found) > 0,
837	            has_greenfield_keywords=len(greenfield_found) > 0,
838	            has_testability_keywords=len(testability_found) > 0,
839	            has_tracking_keywords=len(tracking_found) > 0,
840	            legacy_keywords_found=legacy_found,
841	            greenfield_keywords_found=greenfield_found,
842	            testability_keywords_found=testability_found,
843	            tracking_keywords_found=tracking_found,
844	            target_language=target_language,
845	            complexity=complexity,
846	            estimated_scope=scope,
847	        )

849	    def _determine_route(self, repo: RepoAnalysis, spec: SpecAnalysis) -> Tuple[str, str, str, int, int]:
850	        """Bestimmt die optimale Route basierend auf der Analyse."""
851	
852	        # ── Specialized routes (take priority over general routes) ──
853	
854	        # Testability route: triggered when spec focuses on improving test infrastructure
855	        if spec.has_testability_keywords and len(spec.testability_keywords_found) >= 2:
856	            reason = f"Testability-Keywords: {', '.join(spec.testability_keywords_found[:3])}"
857	            return "testability", reason, "high", 0, 0
858	
859	        # Tracking route: triggered when spec focuses on adding analytics/PostHog
860	        if spec.has_tracking_keywords and len(spec.tracking_keywords_found) >= 2:
861	            reason = f"Tracking-Keywords: {', '.join(spec.tracking_keywords_found[:3])}"
862	            return "tracking", reason, "high", 0, 0
863	
864	        # ── General routes ──
865	
866	        legacy_signals = 0
867	        greenfield_signals = 0
868	        reasons = []
869	
870	        # Legacy Signale
871	        if repo.last_commit_days_ago > 365:
872	            legacy_signals += 2
873	            reasons.append(f"Repo seit {repo.last_commit_days_ago // 365} Jahr(en) inaktiv")
874	
875	        if repo.primary_language in ["Java", "Ruby", "PHP", "Swift", "Objective-C"]:
876	            if spec.target_language and spec.target_language != repo.primary_language:
877	                legacy_signals += 3
878	                reasons.append(f"Sprach-Migration von {repo.primary_language} zu {spec.target_language}")
879	
880	        if spec.has_legacy_keywords:
881	            # Scale by keyword density: 1-2 keywords = partial refactor (+1), 3+ = real migration (+2)
882	            if len(spec.legacy_keywords_found) >= 3:
883	                legacy_signals += 2
884	            else:
885	                legacy_signals += 1
886	            reasons.append(f"Legacy-Keywords in Spec: {', '.join(spec.legacy_keywords_found[:3])}")
887	
888	        if repo.commit_count > 2000:
889	            legacy_signals += 1
890	            reasons.append(f"Großes Projekt mit {repo.commit_count} Commits")
891	
892	        # Greenfield Signale
893	        if spec.has_greenfield_keywords:
894	            greenfield_signals += 2
895	            reasons.append(f"Greenfield-Keywords in Spec: {', '.join(spec.greenfield_keywords_found[:3])}")
896	
897	        if repo.primary_language in ["TypeScript", "JavaScript", "Python", "Go", "Rust"]:
898	            greenfield_signals += 1
899	
900	        if repo.last_commit_days_ago < 90:
901	            greenfield_signals += 1
902	            reasons.append("Aktiv entwickeltes Projekt")
903	
904	        if repo.file_count < 50:
905	            greenfield_signals += 1
906	            reasons.append("Kleines Projekt")
907	
908	        # Route bestimmen
909	        if legacy_signals >= 4:
910	            route = "legacy"
911	            confidence = "high"
912	        elif legacy_signals >= 3:
913	            route = "legacy"
914	            confidence = "medium"
915	        elif greenfield_signals >= 3:
916	            route = "greenfield"
917	            confidence = "high"
918	        elif greenfield_signals >= 2:
919	            route = "greenfield"
920	            confidence = "medium"
921	        else:
922	            route = "hybrid"
923	            confidence = "medium"
924	            reasons.append("Neues Feature auf bestehendem Projekt")
925	
926	        # Reason zusammensetzen
927	        reason = "; ".join(reasons[:3]) if reasons else "Standard-Route"
928	
929	        return route, reason, confidence, legacy_signals, greenfield_signals
930

class Analyzer:
    """Analysiert Repository und Spec für die Routing-Entscheidung."""

    def __init__(self, repo_path: str, spec_path: str):
        self.repo_path = Path(repo_path).resolve()
        self.spec_path = Path(spec_path).resolve()
        self.logger = get_logger()

    def analyze(self) -> AnalysisResult:
        """Führt die komplette Analyse durch."""
        self.logger.analyze(self.repo_path.name)

        repo_analysis = self._analyze_repo()
        spec_analysis = self._analyze_spec()
        runtime_analysis = self._analyze_runtime(repo_analysis)

        # Log Statistiken
        age_info = f"{repo_analysis.last_commit_days_ago} Tage seit letztem Commit"
        if repo_analysis.last_commit_days_ago > 365:
            years = repo_analysis.last_commit_days_ago // 365
            age_info = f"{years} Jahr{'e' if years > 1 else ''} alt"

        self.logger.stats(
            f"{repo_analysis.primary_language}/{repo_analysis.framework_detected or 'kein Framework'}",
            repo_analysis.commit_count,
            age_info
        )

        # Log Runtime-Info
        if runtime_analysis:
            self.logger.debug(
                f"Runtime: {runtime_analysis.start_command} (Port {runtime_analysis.port}), "
                f"{len(runtime_analysis.services)} Services, Doppler: {runtime_analysis.uses_doppler}"
            )

        # Route bestimmen
        route, reason, confidence, legacy_signals, greenfield_signals = self._determine_route(
            repo_analysis, spec_analysis
        )

        self.logger.route(route, confidence)
        self.logger.reason(reason)

        result = AnalysisResult(
            repo=repo_analysis,
            spec=spec_analysis,
            runtime=runtime_analysis,
            route=route,
            route_reason=reason,
            confidence=confidence,
            legacy_signals=legacy_signals,
            greenfield_signals=greenfield_signals,
        )

        # Speichere Analyse
        self._save_analysis(result)

        return result

    def _analyze_repo(self) -> RepoAnalysis:
        """Analysiert die Repository-Struktur."""
        # Dateien sammeln
        all_files = self._get_all_files()

        # Sprachen erkennen
        primary_lang, secondary_langs = self._detect_languages(all_files)

        # Framework erkennen
        framework = self._detect_framework()

        # Build-Tool erkennen
        build_tool = self._detect_build_tool()

        # Git-Statistiken
        commit_count, last_commit_days, last_commit_date = self._get_git_stats()

        # Codezeilen zählen (approximativ)
        total_lines = self._count_lines(all_files)

        # Verzeichnisse
        directories = self._get_top_directories()

        # Config-Dateien
        config_files = self._get_config_files()

        # Tests vorhanden?
        has_tests = self._has_tests()

        # CI vorhanden?
        has_ci = self._has_ci()

        return RepoAnalysis(
            repo_path=str(self.repo_path),
            repo_name=self.repo_path.name,
            primary_language=primary_lang,
            secondary_languages=secondary_langs,
            framework_detected=framework,
            build_tool=build_tool,
            commit_count=commit_count,
            last_commit_days_ago=last_commit_days,
            last_commit_date=last_commit_date,
            file_count=len(all_files),
            total_lines=total_lines,
            has_tests=has_tests,
            has_ci=has_ci,
            directories=directories,
            config_files=config_files,
        )

    def _get_all_files(self) -> List[Path]:
        """Sammelt alle Dateien im Repo (ohne .git, node_modules, etc.)."""
        exclude_dirs = {
            ".git", "node_modules", "vendor", "venv", ".venv",
            "__pycache__", ".idea", ".vscode", "dist", "build",
            "target", ".gradle", ".dirigent",
        }

        files = []
        for root, dirs, filenames in os.walk(self.repo_path):
            # Exclude-Verzeichnisse entfernen
            dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if d not in exclude_dirs]

            for filename in filenames:
                if not filename.startswith("."):
                    files.append(Path(root) / filename)

        return files

    def _detect_languages(self, files: List[Path]) -> Tuple[str, List[str]]:
        """Erkennt primäre und sekundäre Sprachen."""
        lang_counts: Counter = Counter()

        for file in files:
            ext = file.suffix.lower()
            if ext in LANGUAGE_EXTENSIONS:
                lang_counts[LANGUAGE_EXTENSIONS[ext]] += 1

        if not lang_counts:
            return "Unknown", []

        sorted_langs = lang_counts.most_common()
        primary = sorted_langs[0][0]
        secondary = [lang for lang, _ in sorted_langs[1:4]]

        return primary, secondary

    def _detect_framework(self) -> Optional[str]:
        """Erkennt das verwendete Framework."""
        for config_file, frameworks in FRAMEWORK_INDICATORS.items():
            config_path = self.repo_path / config_file
            if config_path.exists():
                try:
                    content = config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()
                    for keyword, framework in frameworks.items():
                        if keyword in content:
                            return framework
                except Exception:
                    pass

        # Spezielle Checks
        if (self.repo_path / "pom.xml").exists():
            try:
                content = (self.repo_path / "pom.xml").read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()
                if "spring-boot" in content or "springframework" in content:
                    return "Spring Boot"
            except Exception:
                pass

        return None

    def _detect_build_tool(self) -> Optional[str]:
        """Erkennt das Build-Tool."""
        for filename, tool in BUILD_TOOLS.items():
            if (self.repo_path / filename).exists():
                return tool
        return None

    def _get_git_stats(self) -> Tuple[int, int, Optional[str]]:
        """Holt Git-Statistiken."""
        try:
            # Commit-Anzahl
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["git", "rev-list", "--count", "HEAD"],
                cwd=self.repo_path,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
            )
            commit_count = int(result.stdout.strip()) if result.returncode == 0 else 0

            # Letzter Commit
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%ci"],
                cwd=self.repo_path,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
            )
            if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
                last_commit_str = result.stdout.strip()
                # Parse: "2024-01-15 10:30:00 +0100"
                last_commit_date = last_commit_str.split()[0]
                last_commit = datetime.fromisoformat(last_commit_date)
                days_ago = (datetime.now() - last_commit).days
                return commit_count, days_ago, last_commit_date

            return commit_count, 0, None

        except Exception:
            return 0, 0, None

    def _count_lines(self, files: List[Path]) -> int:
        """Zählt Codezeilen (approximativ)."""
        total = 0
        code_extensions = set(LANGUAGE_EXTENSIONS.keys())

        for file in files[:500]:  # Limit für Performance
            if file.suffix.lower() in code_extensions:
                try:
                    total += len(file.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines())
                except Exception:
                    pass

        return total

    def _get_top_directories(self) -> List[str]:
        """Gibt die wichtigsten Verzeichnisse zurück."""
        important_dirs = ["src", "app", "lib", "api", "components", "pages",
                         "ios", "android", "server", "client", "core", "modules"]

        found = []
        for d in self.repo_path.iterdir():
            if d.is_dir() and not d.name.startswith("."):
                if d.name in important_dirs:
                    found.append(d.name)

        return found

    def _get_config_files(self) -> List[str]:
        """Gibt gefundene Konfigurationsdateien zurück."""
        config_patterns = [
            "package.json", "pom.xml", "build.gradle", "Cargo.toml",
            "go.mod", "requirements.txt", "Gemfile", "composer.json",
            "tsconfig.json", "webpack.config.js", "vite.config.js",
            ".env", ".env.example", "docker-compose.yml", "Dockerfile",
        ]

        found = []
        for pattern in config_patterns:
            if (self.repo_path / pattern).exists():
                found.append(pattern)

        return found

    def _has_tests(self) -> bool:
        """Prüft ob Tests vorhanden sind."""
        test_indicators = [
            "test", "tests", "spec", "specs", "__tests__",
            "pytest.ini", "jest.config.js", "vitest.config.js",
        ]

        for d in self.repo_path.iterdir():
            if d.name.lower() in test_indicators:
                return True

        return False

    def _has_ci(self) -> bool:
        """Prüft ob CI-Konfiguration vorhanden ist."""
        ci_paths = [
            ".github/workflows",
            ".gitlab-ci.yml",
            ".circleci",
            "Jenkinsfile",
            ".travis.yml",
            "azure-pipelines.yml",
        ]

        for path in ci_paths:
            if (self.repo_path / path).exists():
                return True

        return False

    def _analyze_runtime(self, repo: RepoAnalysis) -> Optional[RuntimeAnalysis]:
        """Analysiert wie das Projekt lokal gestartet werden kann."""
        try:
            start_command = "npm run dev"
            port = 3000
            package_manager = "npm"
            framework = repo.framework_detected
            services: List[RuntimeRequirement] = []
            setup_steps: List[str] = []
            uses_doppler = False
            uses_docker_compose = False
            env_file = None
            health_check_path = None

            # 1. Package Manager und Start-Command aus package.json
            package_json_path = self.repo_path / "package.json"
            if package_json_path.exists():
                try:
                    package_data = json.loads(package_json_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                    scripts = package_data.get("scripts", {})
                    deps = {**package_data.get("dependencies", {}), **package_data.get("devDependencies", {})}

                    # Package Manager erkennen
                    pkg_manager = package_data.get("packageManager", "")
                    if pkg_manager.startswith("yarn"):
                        package_manager = "yarn"
                    elif pkg_manager.startswith("pnpm"):
                        package_manager = "pnpm"
                    elif (self.repo_path / "yarn.lock").exists():
                        package_manager = "yarn"
                    elif (self.repo_path / "pnpm-lock.yaml").exists():
                        package_manager = "pnpm"

                    # Start-Command erkennen
                    if "dev" in scripts:
                        start_command = f"{package_manager} run dev" if package_manager != "npm" else "npm run dev"
                        # Port aus dev script extrahieren
                        port_match = re.search(r"--port[=\s]+(\d+)|-p[=\s]+(\d+)", scripts["dev"])
                        if port_match:
                            port = int(port_match.group(1) or port_match.group(2))
                    elif "start:dev" in scripts:
                        start_command = f"{package_manager} run start:dev" if package_manager != "npm" else "npm run start:dev"
                    elif "serve" in scripts:
                        start_command = f"{package_manager} run serve" if package_manager != "npm" else "npm run serve"

                    # Framework-spezifische Ports
                    if "next" in deps:
                        framework = "Next.js"
                        port = 3000
                    elif "nuxt" in deps:
                        framework = "Nuxt"
                        port = 3000
                    elif "@angular/core" in deps:
                        framework = "Angular"
                        port = 4200
                    elif "vue" in deps and "nuxt" not in deps:
                        framework = "Vue"
                        port = 5173
                    elif "svelte" in deps or "@sveltejs/kit" in deps:
                        framework = "Svelte/SvelteKit"
                        port = 5173
                    elif "astro" in deps:
                        framework = "Astro"
                        port = 4321

                    # Setup-Steps
                    setup_steps.append(f"{package_manager} install")
                    if "db:migrate" in scripts:
                        setup_steps.append(f"{package_manager} run db:migrate")
                    elif "migrate" in scripts:
                        setup_steps.append(f"{package_manager} run migrate")
                    if "db:seed" in scripts:
                        setup_steps.append(f"{package_manager} run db:seed")

                    # Services aus Dependencies erkennen
                    # WICHTIG: Bei Doppler werden externe Services (Supabase, etc.) genutzt
                    # -> Keine lokalen DB-Container starten!
                    if not uses_doppler:
                        if "pg" in deps or "postgres" in deps or "@prisma/client" in deps:
                            services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                                name="PostgreSQL",
                                type="database",
                                docker_image="postgres:15",
                                port=5432,
                                env_vars=["POSTGRES_PASSWORD=dev", "POSTGRES_DB=app_dev"]
                            ))
                        if "mysql" in deps or "mysql2" in deps:
                            services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                                name="MySQL",
                                type="database",
                                docker_image="mysql:8",
                                port=3306,
                                env_vars=["MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=dev", "MYSQL_DATABASE=app_dev"]
                            ))
                        if "mongodb" in deps or "mongoose" in deps:
                            services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                                name="MongoDB",
                                type="database",
                                docker_image="mongo:7",
                                port=27017
                            ))
                        if "redis" in deps or "ioredis" in deps:
                            services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                                name="Redis",
                                type="cache",
                                docker_image="redis:7-alpine",
                                port=6379
                            ))

                except Exception as e:
                    self.logger.debug(f"Error parsing package.json: {e}")

            # 2. Python-Projekte
            elif (self.repo_path / "pyproject.toml").exists() or (self.repo_path / "requirements.txt").exists():
                package_manager = "pip"
                setup_steps.append("pip install -r requirements.txt" if (self.repo_path / "requirements.txt").exists() else "pip install -e .")

                # Framework erkennen
                for req_file in ["requirements.txt", "pyproject.toml"]:
                    req_path = self.repo_path / req_file
                    if req_path.exists():
                        content = req_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()
                        if "fastapi" in content:
                            framework = "FastAPI"
                            start_command = "uvicorn main:app --reload"
                            port = 8000
                        elif "django" in content:
                            framework = "Django"
                            start_command = "python manage.py runserver"
                            port = 8000
                            setup_steps.append("python manage.py migrate")
                        elif "flask" in content:
                            framework = "Flask"
                            start_command = "flask run"
                            port = 5000
                        break

            # 3. Go-Projekte
            elif (self.repo_path / "go.mod").exists():
                package_manager = "go"
                start_command = "go run ."
                port = 8080
                framework = "Go"

            # 4. docker-compose.yml analysieren
            compose_path = self.repo_path / "docker-compose.yml"
            if not compose_path.exists():
                compose_path = self.repo_path / "docker-compose.yaml"

            if compose_path.exists():
                uses_docker_compose = True
                try:
                    compose_content = compose_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()

                    # Services aus docker-compose extrahieren (vereinfacht)
                    if "postgres" in compose_content and not any(s.name == "PostgreSQL" for s in services):
                        services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                            name="PostgreSQL",
                            type="database",
                            docker_image="postgres:15",
                            port=5432,
                            env_vars=["POSTGRES_PASSWORD=dev"]
                        ))
                    if "redis" in compose_content and not any(s.name == "Redis" for s in services):
                        services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                            name="Redis",
                            type="cache",
                            docker_image="redis:7-alpine",
                            port=6379
                        ))
                    if "mongo" in compose_content and not any(s.name == "MongoDB" for s in services):
                        services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                            name="MongoDB",
                            type="database",
                            docker_image="mongo:7",
                            port=27017
                        ))
                    if "elasticsearch" in compose_content:
                        services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                            name="Elasticsearch",
                            type="search",
                            docker_image="elasticsearch:8.11.0",
                            port=9200
                        ))
                    if "rabbitmq" in compose_content:
                        services.append(RuntimeRequirement(
                            name="RabbitMQ",
                            type="queue",
                            docker_image="rabbitmq:3-management",
                            port=5672
                        ))
                except Exception as e:
                    self.logger.debug(f"Error parsing docker-compose: {e}")

            # 5. Doppler-Nutzung erkennen
            doppler_indicators = [".doppler.yaml", "doppler.yaml"]
            for indicator in doppler_indicators:
                if (self.repo_path / indicator).exists():
                    uses_doppler = True
                    break

            # Auch in README oder env.example nach Doppler suchen
            for check_file in ["README.md", ".env.example", "CONTRIBUTING.md"]:
                check_path = self.repo_path / check_file
                if check_path.exists():
                    try:
                        content = check_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").lower()
                        if "doppler" in content:
                            uses_doppler = True
                            break
                    except Exception:
                        pass

            # 6. Env-File finden
            for env_name in [".env.example", ".env.local.example", ".env.sample", ".env.development"]:
                if (self.repo_path / env_name).exists():
                    env_file = env_name
                    break

            # 7. Health-Check-Path erkennen (häufige Patterns)
            health_patterns = ["/api/health", "/health", "/healthz", "/_health"]
            for src_dir in ["src", "app", "api", "."]:
                for pattern in ["health", "healthcheck"]:
                    for ext in [".ts", ".js", ".py", ".go"]:
                        health_file = self.repo_path / src_dir / f"{pattern}{ext}"
                        if health_file.exists():
                            health_check_path = "/api/health"
                            break

            return RuntimeAnalysis(
                start_command=start_command,
                port=port,
                package_manager=package_manager,
                framework=framework,
                services=services,
                setup_steps=setup_steps,
                uses_doppler=uses_doppler,
                uses_docker_compose=uses_docker_compose,
                env_file=env_file,
                health_check_path=health_check_path,
            )

        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.debug(f"Runtime analysis failed: {e}")
            return None

    def _analyze_spec(self) -> SpecAnalysis:
        """Analysiert die Spec-Datei."""
        content = self.spec_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        content_lower = content.lower()

        # Titel extrahieren (erste H1 Zeile)
        title = "Unbekannt"
        for line in content.splitlines():
            if line.startswith("# "):
                title = line[2:].strip()
                break

        # Legacy-Keywords suchen
        legacy_found = []
        for keyword in LEGACY_KEYWORDS:
            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
                legacy_found.append(keyword)

        # Greenfield-Keywords suchen
        greenfield_found = []
        for keyword in GREENFIELD_KEYWORDS:
            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
                greenfield_found.append(keyword)

        # Testability-Keywords suchen
        testability_found = []
        for keyword in TESTABILITY_KEYWORDS:
            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
                testability_found.append(keyword)

        # Tracking-Keywords suchen
        tracking_found = []
        for keyword in TRACKING_KEYWORDS:
            if keyword.lower() in content_lower:
                tracking_found.append(keyword)

        # Target-Language erkennen
        target_language = None
        for keyword, lang in LANGUAGE_KEYWORDS.items():
            if keyword in content_lower:
                target_language = lang
                break

        # Komplexität aus Spec lesen
        complexity = None
        complexity_match = re.search(r"complexity:\s*(\w+)", content_lower)
        if complexity_match:
            complexity = complexity_match.group(1)

        # Scope schätzen
        word_count = len(content.split())
        if word_count < 200:
            scope = "small"
        elif word_count < 500:
            scope = "medium"
        else:
            scope = "large"

        return SpecAnalysis(
            spec_path=str(self.spec_path),
            title=title,
            has_legacy_keywords=len(legacy_found) > 0,
            has_greenfield_keywords=len(greenfield_found) > 0,
            has_testability_keywords=len(testability_found) > 0,
            has_tracking_keywords=len(tracking_found) > 0,
            legacy_keywords_found=legacy_found,
            greenfield_keywords_found=greenfield_found,
            testability_keywords_found=testability_found,
            tracking_keywords_found=tracking_found,
            target_language=target_language,
            complexity=complexity,
            estimated_scope=scope,
        )

    def _determine_route(self, repo: RepoAnalysis, spec: SpecAnalysis) -> Tuple[str, str, str, int, int]:
        """Bestimmt die optimale Route basierend auf der Analyse."""

        # ── Specialized routes (take priority over general routes) ──

        # Testability route: triggered when spec focuses on improving test infrastructure
        if spec.has_testability_keywords and len(spec.testability_keywords_found) >= 2:
            reason = f"Testability-Keywords: {', '.join(spec.testability_keywords_found[:3])}"
            return "testability", reason, "high", 0, 0

        # Tracking route: triggered when spec focuses on adding analytics/PostHog
        if spec.has_tracking_keywords and len(spec.tracking_keywords_found) >= 2:
            reason = f"Tracking-Keywords: {', '.join(spec.tracking_keywords_found[:3])}"
            return "tracking", reason, "high", 0, 0

        # ── General routes ──

        legacy_signals = 0
        greenfield_signals = 0
        reasons = []

        # Legacy Signale
        if repo.last_commit_days_ago > 365:
            legacy_signals += 2
            reasons.append(f"Repo seit {repo.last_commit_days_ago // 365} Jahr(en) inaktiv")

        if repo.primary_language in ["Java", "Ruby", "PHP", "Swift", "Objective-C"]:
            if spec.target_language and spec.target_language != repo.primary_language:
                legacy_signals += 3
                reasons.append(f"Sprach-Migration von {repo.primary_language} zu {spec.target_language}")

        if spec.has_legacy_keywords:
            # Scale by keyword density: 1-2 keywords = partial refactor (+1), 3+ = real migration (+2)
            if len(spec.legacy_keywords_found) >= 3:
                legacy_signals += 2
            else:
                legacy_signals += 1
            reasons.append(f"Legacy-Keywords in Spec: {', '.join(spec.legacy_keywords_found[:3])}")

        if repo.commit_count > 2000:
            legacy_signals += 1
            reasons.append(f"Großes Projekt mit {repo.commit_count} Commits")

        # Greenfield Signale
        if spec.has_greenfield_keywords:
            greenfield_signals += 2
            reasons.append(f"Greenfield-Keywords in Spec: {', '.join(spec.greenfield_keywords_found[:3])}")

        if repo.primary_language in ["TypeScript", "JavaScript", "Python", "Go", "Rust"]:
            greenfield_signals += 1

        if repo.last_commit_days_ago < 90:
            greenfield_signals += 1
            reasons.append("Aktiv entwickeltes Projekt")

        if repo.file_count < 50:
            greenfield_signals += 1
            reasons.append("Kleines Projekt")

        # Route bestimmen
        if legacy_signals >= 4:
            route = "legacy"
            confidence = "high"
        elif legacy_signals >= 3:
            route = "legacy"
            confidence = "medium"
        elif greenfield_signals >= 3:
            route = "greenfield"
            confidence = "high"
        elif greenfield_signals >= 2:
            route = "greenfield"
            confidence = "medium"
        else:
            route = "hybrid"
            confidence = "medium"
            reasons.append("Neues Feature auf bestehendem Projekt")

        # Reason zusammensetzen
        reason = "; ".join(reasons[:3]) if reasons else "Standard-Route"

        return route, reason, confidence, legacy_signals, greenfield_signals

    def _save_analysis(self, result: AnalysisResult):
        """Speichert das Analyse-Ergebnis."""
        dirigent_dir = self.repo_path / ".dirigent"
        dirigent_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        analysis_file = dirigent_dir / "ANALYSIS.json"

        # Dataclass zu Dict konvertieren
        data = {
            "repo_path": result.repo.repo_path,
            "repo_name": result.repo.repo_name,
            "primary_language": result.repo.primary_language,
            "secondary_languages": result.repo.secondary_languages,
            "framework_detected": result.repo.framework_detected,
            "build_tool": result.repo.build_tool,
            "commit_count": result.repo.commit_count,
            "last_commit_days_ago": result.repo.last_commit_days_ago,
            "file_count": result.repo.file_count,
            "total_lines": result.repo.total_lines,
            "spec_title": result.spec.title,
            "spec_keywords": result.spec.legacy_keywords_found + result.spec.greenfield_keywords_found,
            "route": result.route,
            "route_reason": result.route_reason,
            "confidence": result.confidence,
            "legacy_signals": result.legacy_signals,
            "greenfield_signals": result.greenfield_signals,
            "analyzed_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        }

        # Runtime-Analyse hinzufügen
        if result.runtime:
            data["runtime"] = {
                "start_command": result.runtime.start_command,
                "port": result.runtime.port,
                "package_manager": result.runtime.package_manager,
                "framework": result.runtime.framework,
                "services": [
                    {
                        "name": s.name,
                        "type": s.type,
                        "docker_image": s.docker_image,
                        "port": s.port,
                        "env_vars": s.env_vars,
                    }
                    for s in result.runtime.services
                ],
                "setup_steps": result.runtime.setup_steps,
                "uses_doppler": result.runtime.uses_doppler,
                "uses_docker_compose": result.runtime.uses_docker_compose,
                "env_file": result.runtime.env_file,
                "health_check_path": result.runtime.health_check_path,
            }

        with open(analysis_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

        self.logger.debug(f"Analyse gespeichert in {analysis_file}")


def load_analysis(repo_path: str) -> Optional[Dict]:
    """Lädt eine existierende Analyse."""
    analysis_file = Path(repo_path) / ".dirigent" / "ANALYSIS.json"
    if analysis_file.exists():
        with open(analysis_file, encoding="utf-8") as f:
            return json.load(f)
    return None

1	"""
2	Task runner — executes individual Claude Code tasks.
